revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:壱岐古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Iki Kofun Group | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun|ja|鬼の窟古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の岩屋古墳}}, part of the Iki Kofun Group | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|qid=Q861519|lt=Iki, Nagasaki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|48|05|N|129|42|09|E}} | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} | mounds_count = 6 | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Iki Kofun Group | map_dot_label = bottom | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Historic Site = {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}} }} '''Iki Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a collection of six Kofuns located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|qid=Q861519|lt=Iki, Nagasaki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}.
== Overview == Situated in central {{ill|Iki Island|en|Iki Island|qid=Q494914|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Island}}, the group consists of six large Kofuns, including two {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and four {{ill|circular Kofuns|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, which are as follows: * {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 63 meters). * {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture). * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Circular mound (diameter: 66 meters). * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126604917}} - Circular mound (diameter: 54 meters). * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Circular mound (diameter: 45 meters).
The construction of the Iki Kofun Group began in the late 6th century with {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|qq=}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, and later transitioned to circular mounds by the end of the century. burials continued into the 7th century. The group represents the lineage of influential chiefs in Iki Island's history. The burial goods include items from China and {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, indicating active exchanges with East Asia during this period.
== Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} (Nationally Designated) === * **burial goods from {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}** (archaeological materials) - Designated on June 8, 2007. ** Includes gold and bronze ornaments, silver items, and pottery. * **burial goods from {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}** (archaeological materials) - Designated on July 10, 2008. ** Includes gold and silver burial goods, iron tools, and glass items.
=== National Historic sites === * {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** Includes {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on February 12, 2009.
=== Iki City Designated Historic site === * **Tangible designation1** ** House-shaped Sarcophagus from {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} (stone artifact).
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Nagasaki) - Houses burial goods from the Iki Kofun Group.
== notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == {{small|（Sources used in this article）}} * Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database * Iki City Historic site Reports
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database]
